bins.name: Flexible Load Fast Track
bins.desc: Emerald AI strongly supports creating a dedicated FLFT that expedites interconnection for loads that commit to verifiable, software-defined flexibility. The filing argues the rule must adopt a technology-neutral, performance-based definition of curtailment so that software-orchestrated demand reduction counts as valid curtailment. It also recommends operator control be satisfied by a secure, auditable software interface rather than physical disconnection.
quotes: We are particularly supportive of Principle 7 and urge the Commission to create a "Flexible Load Fast Track" (FLFT) that rewards verifiable flexibility with what large loads value most: faster and larger interconnections for new facilities seeking swift access and existing facilities seeking larger and speedy capacity to upgrade to latest-generation computing technology.

The Commission should instead adopt a performance-based definition of flexibility, consistent with the shift toward performance-based standards seen in other areas of grid regulation. If a facility can demonstrate, through telemetry and secure software interfaces, that it will reduce its withdrawal from 100 MW to 75 MW within the required timeframe of a signal, it effectively provides the same reliability value as a physical breaker, but with vastly higher economic efficiency.
The final rule should be technology-neutral and performance-based, recognizing the full spectrum of on-site flexibility.
Emerald AI strongly recommends this be defined as a secure, standardized, and auditable software interface, not direct, physical utility control.

Annual Event Limits: Setting a reasonable ceiling on annual curtailment events prevents excessive dispatch and ensures that flexible loads are used strategically, during genuine system stress, rather than as routine operational tools. AI data centers and next-generation AI Factories engage in the most economically critical workloads of the entire economy, so their curtailment should be extremely judicious.

bins.name: Expedited Study for Flexible Loads
bins.desc: Emerald AI supports a fast-track study path with a 60 to 90 day approval timeline for loads that demonstrate verifiable flexibility thresholds. It argues that performance-based software verification provides the same reliability signal as physical control, justifying the expedited treatment.
quotes: We are particularly supportive of Principle 7 and urge the Commission to create a "Flexible Load Fast Track" (FLFT) that rewards verifiable flexibility with what large loads value most: faster and larger interconnections for new facilities seeking swift access and existing facilities seeking larger and speedy capacity to upgrade to latest-generation computing technology.

The Commission should instead adopt a performance-based definition of flexibility, consistent with the shift toward performance-based standards seen in other areas of grid regulation. If a facility can demonstrate, through telemetry and secure software interfaces, that it will reduce its withdrawal from 100 MW to 75 MW within the required timeframe of a signal, it effectively provides the same reliability value as a physical breaker, but with vastly higher economic efficiency.
Emerald AI strongly recommends this be defined as a secure, standardized, and auditable software interface, not direct, physical utility control.

the FLFT would allow facilities meeting defined flexibility thresholds to receive interconnection approval within 60 to 90 days, based on verification of available firm capacity and demonstration of control and telemetry systems.

bins.name: Study Process Reform
bins.desc: The filing contends that the current study process forces flexible loads to be evaluated at worst-case inflexible peak, overstating required upgrades. Fixing this requires a parallel FLFT study track with a 60 to 90 day timeline and the ability for curtailable customers to be studied against their committed net withdrawal profile.
quotes: The greatest opportunity in this rulemaking is not merely streamlining the administrative study process, but enabling large loads to actively avoid or defer massive grid and energy infrastructure upgrades.

Transmission service frameworks do not yet exist to allow for load flexibility to avoid network upgrades. Network Integration Transmission Service at the wholesale level is non-interruptible under FERC's pro forma tariff. And, while non-firm point-to-point service does exist, there is no non-firm network option for a load to access and draw upon the full network during times it is not curtailed. Nor do utilities studying large load interconnection generally provide a pathway through which a curtailable customer can be studied to avoid network upgrades.

the FLFT would allow facilities meeting defined flexibility thresholds to receive interconnection approval within 60 to 90 days, based on verification of available firm capacity and demonstration of control and telemetry systems.

bins.name: Avoid Unnecessary Upgrade Costs
bins.desc: Emerald AI argues the near-zero marginal cost of software flexibility makes it far cheaper than new transmission, and that the existing tariff structure forces ratepayers to subsidize upgrades that could be avoided. The filing supports directing upgrade costs to loads that remain inflexible and exempting those that deliver verified flexibility.
quotes: The greatest opportunity in this rulemaking is not merely streamlining the administrative study process, but enabling large loads to actively avoid or defer massive grid and energy infrastructure upgrades.
the marginal cost of software-based flexibility is near-zero (operating expense), whereas the marginal cost of new transmission capacity is extremely high (capital expense).

Transmission service frameworks do not yet exist to allow for load flexibility to avoid network upgrades. Network Integration Transmission Service at the wholesale level is non-interruptible under FERC's pro forma tariff. And, while non-firm point-to-point service does exist, there is no non-firm network option for a load to access and draw upon the full network during times it is not curtailed. Nor do utilities studying large load interconnection generally provide a pathway through which a curtailable customer can be studied to avoid network upgrades.

projects that remain inflexible proceed through the standard process and may bear upgrade-related costs, whereas projects that commit to and deliver flexibility can avoid or reduce those upgrades and connect sooner.

bins.name: Upgrade Cost Tied to Flexibility
bins.desc: Emerald AI supports tying upgrade cost responsibility to flexibility performance: inflexible loads bear upgrade costs while flexible loads avoid them. It frames software-based flexibility as a low-cost substitute for capital-intensive transmission, protecting ratepayers from unnecessary socialization of those costs.
quotes: the marginal cost of software-based flexibility is near-zero (operating expense), whereas the marginal cost of new transmission capacity is extremely high (capital expense).

projects that remain inflexible proceed through the standard process and may bear upgrade-related costs, whereas projects that commit to and deliver flexibility can avoid or reduce those upgrades and connect sooner.

bins.name: PJM Spatial Flexibility Example
bins.desc: Emerald AI cites PJM as an example of a congested region where workload migration to a less constrained area such as MISO can act as a virtual transmission line, relieving local constraints without new infrastructure.
quotes: If the PJM interconnection is thermally constrained but the MISO region has excess wind generation, an orchestrator can route new inference requests to the MISO facility, for example. This capability acts as a "virtual transmission line." Instead of moving megawatts of electrons across a congested wire to meet a static demand, the system moves the demand for those electrons to a location where power is abundant.

bins.name: MISO as Flexibility Destination
bins.desc: MISO is cited as a region with surplus wind generation that could absorb AI workloads shifted from a constrained PJM interconnection, illustrating the value of cross-region spatial flexibility.
quotes: If the PJM interconnection is thermally constrained but the MISO region has excess wind generation, an orchestrator can route new inference requests to the MISO facility, for example. This capability acts as a "virtual transmission line." Instead of moving megawatts of electrons across a congested wire to meet a static demand, the system moves the demand for those electrons to a location where power is abundant.

bins.name: Cross-Region Workload Migration
bins.desc: Emerald AI describes migrating AI compute workloads across data centers in different regions via fiber networks as a non-wires alternative to building new transmission. It frames this spatial flexibility as a virtual transmission line that moves demand to where power is available rather than moving electrons across congested wires.
quotes: If the PJM interconnection is thermally constrained but the MISO region has excess wind generation, an orchestrator can route new inference requests to the MISO facility, for example. This capability acts as a "virtual transmission line." Instead of moving megawatts of electrons across a congested wire to meet a static demand, the system moves the demand for those electrons to a location where power is abundant.

bins.name: No Tariff Path for Flexible Loads
bins.desc: The filing identifies a structural gap in existing wholesale tariff frameworks: no transmission service product allows a curtailable load to avoid network upgrade costs, because both network integration service and point-to-point non-firm service lack the flexibility pathway needed. This gap causes overestimation of required upgrades.
quotes: Transmission service frameworks do not yet exist to allow for load flexibility to avoid network upgrades. Network Integration Transmission Service at the wholesale level is non-interruptible under FERC's pro forma tariff. And, while non-firm point-to-point service does exist, there is no non-firm network option for a load to access and draw upon the full network during times it is not curtailed. Nor do utilities studying large load interconnection generally provide a pathway through which a curtailable customer can be studied to avoid network upgrades.

bins.name: Technology-Neutral Flexibility Rule
bins.desc: Emerald AI asks FERC to avoid prescribing specific hardware or methods for meeting flexibility commitments, instead setting measurable performance criteria that allow software-only, hardware-only, or hybrid solutions to qualify.
quotes: The final rule should be technology-neutral and performance-based, recognizing the full spectrum of on-site flexibility.

bins.name: Structured Curtailment Event Limits
bins.desc: Emerald AI proposes six performance criteria categories including event duration, advance notice, recovery periods, annual event limits, response time tolerances, and testing rules. It specifically requests annual event limits to prevent excessive dispatch of data centers whose workloads represent critical economic infrastructure.
quotes: Annual Event Limits: Setting a reasonable ceiling on annual curtailment events prevents excessive dispatch and ensures that flexible loads are used strategically, during genuine system stress, rather than as routine operational tools. AI data centers and next-generation AI Factories engage in the most economically critical workloads of the entire economy, so their curtailment should be extremely judicious.
